meaning: vt.花费超过...范围 比(他人)花费多
examples: European investors may outspend the Japanese this year.
Over the past decade, Americans were able to outspend their incomes by easily rolling their debts forward through serial home refinancing.
Previously, the best teams could outspend the worst ones on amateur talent without penalty.
Mr Perry won plaudits as commissioner of agriculture, and should be able to outspend Mr Sharp.
"Chris Christie is going to far outspend his Democratic opponent, every Democrat knows that, " Mr. Dworkin said.
